name: Tortilla Soup-Texas Home Cooking
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup red-ripe tomatoes, chopped, seeded or canned crushed
1 medium onion, 1/2 chopped and 1/2 sliced into thin rings
1 fresh jalapeno, sliced into thin rings
1 chipotle chile (whole if dried, or minced if canned)
2 cups shredded cooked chicken or turkey
4 corn tortillas, sliced into thin strips, toasted or broiled until crisp
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy saucepan, bring the stock to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered until the stock reduces to approximately 6 cups.
3. Add garlic, tomatoes, chopped half of onion, jalapeno, chipotle, and oregano to the stock. Continue simmering uncovered over medium-low heat for another 30 minutes.
4. While the soup cooks, place remaining onions on a greased baking sheet and broil until softened and lightly browned around edges. Add these onions, chicken, and lime juice to the soup; continue simmering until chicken is heated through.
5. If using dried chipotle, remove chile and mince it or discard it based on desired spice level.
6. To serve, place a small handful of totilla strips in each bowl and pour the soup over. Pass garnishes separately.
Roasted garlic: Roast individual cloves with skins on in a heavy skillet over low heat until skins darken all around and cloves soften.
NOTES: Tortilla soup originated in Mexico as a way to use old tortillas and leftover poultry, but it developed into one of the classics of national cuisine. For full flavor, don't skip roasting garlic or broiling onions; these steps add smoky mellowness that can be enhanced with smoked poultry.
